description: The v2 additions to the safety taxonomy: a controlled organ-system vocabulary, and the rule that it is READ from a source rather than inferred. A NEW file, not an edit of safety_taxonomy_v1.json, which is bound by hash into every release ever emitted.

hard_rules: An organ system is READ from a source and bound to the exact place in it that says so. A classification with no locator is not a classification.

An organ system is NEVER inferred from target biology, gene expression, drug class, mechanism, or any Stage-1/2/3 signal. 'The target is an immune gene, therefore the toxicity is immune/infectious' is a guess about biology wearing the costume of a label finding.
There is deliberately no 'inferred_*' member of extraction_status. The vocabulary IS the enforcement: an inference cannot be written down, so it cannot be emitted, so nothing downstream can read one.
extraction_status='not_stated_in_source' requires organ_system='unspecified'. When the source is silent the answer is unspecified, not the organ system the biology would suggest.
A safety row with evidence_state 'not_evaluated' or 'no_evidence_found' has no finding to classify and may not carry an organ system.

An organ-system classification is DESCRIPTIVE. It is never counted, scored, ranked, weighted, or aggregated into any number, and it never gates or reorders any PK, exposure, transporter, NEBPI or CNS-MPO lane.
An incomplete or unspecified organ-system classification never blocks or downgrades any other lane. A missing category is a missing category, not a penalty.

prohibited_outputs_v2.statement: In addition to the v1 forbidden field names, which remain in force. Stage 4 emits no combined verdict of any kind.
prohibited_outputs_v2.reason: A p/q value here would imply a hypothesis test this stage does not run, and an organ-system score would recreate the single combined clinical verdict the whole taxonomy exists to prevent. The organ system is a label on a finding, not a quantity.
